text.title: react-hooks Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for react-hooks as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Calling a hook inside an if statement, loop, or after an early return.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: React tracks hook state by call order. Skipping a hook call on one render shifts every subsequent hook's slot, corrupting state in non-obvious ways that appear as stale values or infinite renders.

failure-mode.description: Missing dependency in useEffect: setCount(count + 1) inside an effect with empty deps [].
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: count is captured at mount time (stale closure). The interval or effect always sees count = 0 no matter how many times it fires. Use functional update setCount(c => c + 1) or add count to deps and clean up on change.

failure-mode.description: Passing an object or array literal as a useEffect dependency: useEffect(..., [{ page: 1 }]).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A new object is created on every render. {} !== {} in JavaScript. The effect fires on every render, creating an infinite loop. Destructure to primitives or memoize the object.

failure-mode.description: Using useEffect to sync derived state: setFullName(first + " " + last) inside an effect.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Creates a second render cycle: props change, render, effect fires, state set, render again. Compute fullName during render directly—no state or effect needed.

failure-mode.description: Defining a styled component or computing a large constant inside the function body.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any value defined inside render is recreated on every render. styled.div inside a component creates a new class on every render, causing style explosion and potentially infinite re-renders.

failure-mode.description: Using useEffect for data fetching without abort cleanup in a component that can unmount or whose props change.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without abort, a response from a previous URL arrives after the URL changed and overwrites the correct data. In Strict Mode, the component unmounts and remounts in dev, causing the same double-fetch issue.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The suppressed warning is a stale closure waiting to manifest. Every suppression is a future production bug. Refactor to eliminate the dependency legitimately instead.
